User Manual & Troubleshooting Guide
EntryPassSync v1.1.2 — EntryPass–MiHCM Attendance Integration
Prepared for:Petra Energy Sdn Bhd — IT Department
Prepared by:Dajayana Trading
Date:March 2026
App Version:v1.1.2
Document Reference:DA/PETRA/MIHCM/UG/2026/002

1. Overview

This guide covers the daily operation, monitoring, and troubleshooting of the EntryPassSync application deployed at Petra Energy sites. It is intended for site IT staff responsible for keeping the system running.

What EntryPassSync Does

  • Reads employee attendance (clock-in/out) data directly from the EntryPass Firebird database
  • Automatically uploads records to MiHCM's cloud HR platform every 15 minutes
  • Starts automatically when Windows starts — runs quietly in the system tray
  • Requires no daily action from IT staff under normal operation

Your Sites

Site Code Location Status
PEMOMiri — Pujut Office✓ Active
PPOMiri — Piasau OfficePre-configured, Ready to Deploy
PFYLabuan — Fabrication YardPre-configured, Ready to Deploy
BIFBintuluPre-configured, Ready to Deploy
SK407Miri — SK407 OfficePre-configured, Ready to Deploy

2. Installation

Each site receives a pre-configured folder from Dajayana Trading. All credentials and settings are already set up — site IT staff only need to copy and launch.

2.1 What's in the Folder

EntryPassSync\
    ├── EntryPassSync.vbs      — Double-click this to launch the app
    ├── EntryPassSync.ps1     — Main application (DO NOT EDIT)
    ├── config.json            — Pre-configured settings (DO NOT EDIT)
    └── dajayana.ico           — Application icon

2.2 First-Time Setup

  1. Copy the EntryPassSync folder to the designated workstation — the same machine that runs EntryPass, or one with network access to the Firebird database server
  2. Right-click EntryPassSync.vbs and select Run as Administrator — this is required for the first launch only, to register the auto-start entry in Windows
  3. The application will open, perform an immediate sync, and begin counting down to the next sync
  4. From this point, the application starts automatically whenever Windows starts. No further setup is required
ⓘ After the first run: You can launch the app normally by double-clicking EntryPassSync.vbs (no Administrator required). The first-run-as-admin is only needed once to set up the Windows auto-start.

3. The Dashboard

When EntryPassSync opens, you see the Dashboard — the main status screen. This is your at-a-glance view of the sync health.

Figure 1 — Dashboard view showing sync status, counters, and next sync countdown

Dashboard Elements Explained

Status Card Shows the current state: Idle (waiting for next sync), Syncing… (upload in progress), or Error (last sync had a problem). Green = all good.
Uploaded Counter Total records successfully sent to MiHCM in the current session. This is your primary health indicator — it should increase with each sync cycle that finds new records.
Failed Counter Records that could not be uploaded. Under normal operation this should be 0. If it shows a non-zero value, check the Sync Log tab for details.
Next Sync Countdown MM:SS timer counting down to the next automatic sync. Resets to 15:00 after each sync completes. If a sync is in progress, this shows "Syncing…"
Site Info Displays your site code (e.g. PEMO) and the configured data source mode. Confirms you are running the correct site configuration.
Sync Now Button Triggers an immediate manual sync without waiting for the 15-minute timer. Useful after a known outage or when you want to confirm records have been uploaded.
Minimising the app: Clicking the window's X (close) button minimises the application to the system tray — it does not stop the sync. Look for the Dajayana logo icon in the bottom-right of your taskbar. Right-click it to open the app again or exit completely.

4. Configuration Panel

Click the Configuration tab to view the data source settings. Most settings are pre-configured and do not need to be changed. Contact Dajayana Trading before modifying anything here.

Figure 2 — Configuration panel showing data source selection and database path

Configuration Options

Data Source Database Direct (default and recommended) — reads live data from the Firebird database. File Mode — fallback option that reads exported text files. Use File Mode only if instructed by Dajayana Trading.
Database Path Full path to the EntryPass TRANS.FDB database file. Pre-configured per site. Only change this if the EntryPass database has been moved to a new location.
Sync Days How many days back to include in each sync (default: 2 — today and yesterday). This ensures records are not missed over weekends or if the app was offline.
API Keys / Credentials MiHCM API credentials, Firebird login, and the license key are stored in config.json and are not shown in the UI. These are set by Dajayana Trading.
⚠ Important: The config.json file contains all credentials pre-configured by Dajayana Trading. Do not edit this file. If you suspect a configuration issue, contact Dajayana Trading for support.

5. Sync Log

Click the Sync Log tab to see a live log of all sync activity. This is where you go when you need to check what happened during a sync, investigate a failure, or confirm that records were uploaded.

Figure 3 — Sync Log tab showing real-time upload activity, record counts, and any errors

Reading the Log

  • Each sync cycle is separated by a divider line with the date and time
  • Look for Uploaded: lines — these confirm records sent to MiHCM successfully
  • Skipped (duplicate) entries are normal — records already in MiHCM are silently ignored
  • Lines containing ERROR or FAILED indicate a problem — note the message and contact Dajayana Trading if it persists
  • The log auto-scrolls to the latest entry during an active sync

Sample Log Output (Successful Sync)

── Sync started: 2026-03-19 08:00:01 ──────────────────
Data source : Database Direct (TRANS.FDB)
Sync window : 2026-03-18 to 2026-03-19
Records found : 186
Batch 1/3 : 80 uploaded
Batch 2/3 : 80 uploaded
Batch 3/3 : 26 uploaded
Duplicates : 4 skipped
Failed : 0
Duration : 00:00:48
── Sync complete ───────────────────────────────────────

6. About & Checking for Updates

Click the About tab to see the application version and to check for updates from Dajayana Trading. Updates are delivered automatically via GitHub — the app checks on every startup, and you can also check manually at any time.

Figure 4 — About panel showing version information and the Check for Updates button

Checking for Updates

  1. Click the About tab
  2. Click the Check for Updates button
  3. If a new version is available, you will be prompted to download it. Click Yes to proceed
  4. The new version replaces the existing EntryPassSync.ps1 — no reinstallation needed
  5. The application will restart automatically after the update
ⓘ Note: The app also checks for updates automatically each time it starts. If a new version is found at startup, you will be prompted before any update is applied. You can decline and continue with the current version.

7. Running a Manual Sync

The application syncs automatically every 15 minutes. However, you can trigger an immediate sync at any time — for example, after a network outage, or to confirm records are flowing after initial deployment.

  1. Open the application (double-click the system tray icon, or launch EntryPassSync.vbs)
  2. On the Dashboard, click the Sync Now button
  3. The status card will change to Syncing… and the countdown timer will pause
  4. Switch to the Sync Log tab to watch progress in real time
  5. When complete, the status returns to Idle and the 15-minute timer resets
  6. Check the log for the uploaded/failed count to confirm success

8. File Locations & Structure

All application files live in the one folder provided by Dajayana Trading. The app creates a log file in the same folder automatically.

EntryPassSync\ (your site's folder)
    ├── EntryPassSync.vbs      — Launcher (double-click to start)
    ├── EntryPassSync.ps1     — Main application (DO NOT EDIT)
    ├── config.json            — Pre-configured settings (DO NOT EDIT)
    ├── dajayana.ico           — Application icon
    └── EntryPassSync.log     — Log file (auto-created, append-only)
Log file management: EntryPassSync.log grows over time as syncs accumulate. It is safe to archive or delete it periodically — the application will create a new one automatically. Recommended: archive monthly by renaming it (e.g., EntryPassSync-202603.log) and moving it to an archive folder.

9. Troubleshooting

Find your symptom below. Each entry shows the likely cause and steps to resolve it.

9.1 Application does not start / window closes immediately
Cause:PowerShell execution policy blocking the script, or a missing file in the folder
Fix:Ensure you are launching via EntryPassSync.vbs — not by double-clicking the .ps1 file directly. Confirm all four files are present in the folder. If the issue persists, right-click EntryPassSync.vbs and select Run as Administrator.
9.2 Status shows "Error" — Cannot connect to database
Cause:The Firebird database service is not running, or the database path in config.json is incorrect
Fix:
  1. Check that the EntryPass server / Firebird service is running (look in Windows Services for "Firebird Server")
  2. If EntryPass itself is working normally, the Firebird service is likely fine — check that the database file path has not changed
  3. If the path has changed, contact Dajayana Trading to update config.json
9.3 "License validation failed" on startup
Cause:Cannot reach the license server — internet connectivity issue, or license has expired
Fix:If the internet was temporarily down, the app will use the local license cache and continue operating normally. No action needed for brief outages. If the error persists for more than a day with internet available, contact Dajayana Trading — the license may need renewal.
9.4 "Cannot proceed without a valid token" / Authentication failed
Cause:MiHCM API authentication failed — internet is down, or API credentials have expired
Fix:
  1. Check internet connectivity (try opening a website)
  2. Wait for the next automatic sync — the app retries automatically
  3. If the error persists across multiple sync cycles, contact Dajayana Trading — API credentials may need renewal
9.5 Failed counter is non-zero — some records not uploading
Cause:API errors, network interruption during upload, or data format issue
Fix:Check the Sync Log tab for the specific error message. The app retries automatically — if the next sync cycle shows 0 failed, the issue was transient. If failures persist, note the error text and contact Dajayana Trading with a copy of the log.
9.6 Log shows "Skipped (duplicate)" records
Cause:These records were already uploaded in a previous sync cycle
Fix:✓ This is normal — it is not an error. The system automatically prevents duplicate records in MiHCM. Duplicates are expected whenever the same day is included in the sync window across multiple runs.
9.7 HTTP 429 — Rate limited
Cause:Too many requests sent to MiHCM API in a short period (rare)
Fix:The application handles this automatically with built-in retry logic and respects the Retry-After header. No action needed — the sync will complete on retry.
9.8 HTTP 500 / 502 / 503 — MiHCM server errors
Cause:MiHCM's servers are temporarily unavailable
Fix:The application retries automatically (up to 3 times with increasing delays). If all retries fail, the sync is skipped and will be retried at the next 15-minute interval. No data is lost — records remain in the Firebird database and will be picked up on the next successful sync.
9.9 Uploaded counter is 0 after multiple syncs
Cause:No new records in the Firebird database, all records already uploaded (shown as duplicates), or a database connection issue
Fix:Check the Sync Log for the "Records found" count. If it shows 0 records found, the database may be empty for the sync window, or EntryPass is not writing new data. Confirm EntryPass card readers are active and recording swipes. If records found is >0 but uploaded is 0, check for error messages in the log.
9.10 Application not in system tray after Windows restart
Cause:Auto-start registry entry was not created (first launch may not have been run as Administrator)
Fix:Right-click EntryPassSync.vbs and select Run as Administrator. This re-registers the auto-start entry. After this, the app will start automatically on every Windows login.

10. When to Contact Dajayana Trading

⚠ Contact Dajayana Trading

  • Authentication fails repeatedly across multiple sync cycles
  • Non-zero failed count persists after several retries
  • Database connection errors persist after confirming Firebird is running
  • Application needs to be deployed at a new site
  • EntryPass has been upgraded or the database moved
  • MiHCM API or subscription changes
  • You need to modify any setting in config.json

✓ You Can Handle This Yourself

  • Re-triggering a sync after a network outage (click Sync Now)
  • Checking the Sync Log for upload status
  • Archiving old log files
  • Clicking "Check for Updates" in the About tab
  • Re-running as Administrator to restore auto-start
